extraction.article_title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621

ttps.description: Exploitation for Client Execution via malicious PDF content that triggers JavaScript execution in Adobe Acrobat Reader
Scripting: abuse of embedded JavaScript handling in a PDF reader

attack_chain: Attacker crafts a malicious PDF document containing specially structured object data and embedded JavaScript-triggering content.
Victim opens the PDF in Adobe Acrobat Reader, causing the parser to process attacker-controlled objects.
A prototype pollution condition alters JavaScript object behavior inside the application runtime.
Polluted object properties influence code flow and can lead to arbitrary JavaScript execution and potentially further code execution.
Attacker delivers a payload through social engineering, phishing, or document distribution.

proposed_vectors.name_en: Malicious PDF object pollution leading to script execution
proposed_vectors.name_ko: 악성 PDF 객체 오염을 통한 스크립트 실행

proposed_phase_updates: Add detection for suspicious PDF JavaScript objects, malformed array/object fields, and unexpected property inheritance patterns in PDFs opened by Acrobat Reader.
Add telemetry for PDF documents that trigger script execution immediately upon open without user interaction.

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: This is not a classic browser XSS, but a client-side script execution primitive in a PDF viewer. A crafted PDF can inject JavaScript-bearing structures or manipulate object prototypes so that the viewer's JavaScript context resolves attacker-controlled properties and executes code paths on document open.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
%PDF-1.7
1 0 obj
<< /Type /Catalog /OpenAction 2 0 R >>
endobj
2 0 obj
<< /S /JavaScript /JS (app.alert('POC: PDF JS executed');) >>
endobj
trailer
<< /Root 1 0 R >>
%%EOF
detect: Alert dialog or any visible script execution when the PDF is opened
Reader process spawns unexpected child processes or accesses scripting APIs
Document open event triggers JavaScript without user interaction

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: Prototype pollution can be used to alter runtime object behavior and redirect execution into attacker-controlled JavaScript. In a vulnerable PDF parser/viewer, malformed nested objects or inherited properties may cause the engine to read poisoned values that alter control flow and potentially enable code execution.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
%PDF-1.7
1 0 obj
<< /Type /Catalog /Names 2 0 R >>
endobj
2 0 obj
<< /JavaScript << /Names [(foo) 3 0 R] >> >>
endobj
3 0 obj
<< /S /JavaScript /JS (var o={}; o.__proto__.polluted='yes'; app.alert(o.polluted);) >>
endobj
trailer
<< /Root 1 0 R >>
%%EOF
detect: Unexpected access to poisoned prototype-derived properties
JavaScript executes on open and displays 'yes' or similar proof text
Crashes, memory corruption, or abnormal reader behavior after document load
